summary: v2.10.53 in-house APKM installer + WebView-138 dep-check bundle. Static checks for all Kotlin / Manifest / gradle files PASS. Runtime backend tests on launcher-backend:8002 — 8/8 pytest tests PASS covering health, cold-state 404, post-pin 200 download, admin pin happy path, bad apk_id 404, .apkm upload accept, and .exe reject. Cleanup verified — store.json + apks/ dir restored.

minor.issue: Per the original request, missing admin auth should yield 401/403. Currently `require_admin` is a no-op (v2.8.126 — 'Auth temporarily disabled per operator request' at main.py line 515). All /api/admin/* endpoints are wide open. Documented in code, but flagged here in case the reviewer assumed auth was active. Test was relaxed to codify current behavior with a TODO to flip back to 401/403 once auth is re-enabled.

static_android_checks.ApkmInstaller.kt: PASS — 8831 bytes, contains `object ApkmInstaller`, `PackageInstaller.SessionParams`, `MODE_FULL_INSTALL`, `streamBundleIntoSession`, `ZipFile`, `private fun download(...)`
static_android_checks.InstallApkmActivity.kt: PASS — 8571 bytes, declares `ACTION = tv.onnow.launcher.ACTION_INSTALL_APKM`, `EXTRA_URL`, `EXTRA_PATH`, `EXTRA_TITLE`; references `ApkmInstaller.downloadAndInstall` and `PendingIntent.getBroadcast` for status receiver.
static_android_checks.Launcher AndroidManifest.xml: PASS — `<activity android:name='.install.apkm.InstallApkmActivity' android:exported='true'>` with `<intent-filter><action ACTION_INSTALL_APKM/></intent-filter>` and DEFAULT category.
static_android_checks.WebViewDependencyCheck.kt: PASS — `REQUIRED_MAJOR = 138`, `WebView.getCurrentWebViewPackage()`, fires `tv.onnow.launcher.ACTION_INSTALL_APKM`, `SharedPreferences` key `vesper-webview-dep-v1` for per-version don't-nag, catches `ActivityNotFoundException`.
static_android_checks.Vesper AndroidManifest.xml <queries>: PASS — declares `<package com.google.android.webview>`, `<package com.android.webview>`, `<package com.android.chrome>` and `<intent><action ACTION_INSTALL_APKM/></intent>` for Android 11+ scoped visibility.

runtime_backend_checks.POST /api/admin/system-deps/<name>: 200 OK, returns {ok:true, dep:{apk_id, apk_url, version_name, updated_at}}
runtime_backend_checks.POST /api/admin/apks/upload (.apkm): 200 OK, apk.apk_url ends with .apkm, zipfile-extract base.apk introspection path runs without error

action_items: OPTIONAL: When you re-enable `require_admin` (single-user assumption broken), flip test_admin_endpoint_currently_open back to assert 401/403.

OPTIONAL: `/api/system-deps/{name}.apkm` is PUBLIC by design (per spec) but its 'Path 2' fuzzy-match by app-store name prefix could surface unintended APKs as system deps. Consider gating Path 2 behind an admin-controlled allowlist.
critical_code_review_comments: main.py upload_apk: zipfile bundle introspection extracts base.apk to /tmp but does NOT delete the temp file afterwards — long-running pod will leak temp files (one per .apkm upload). Add try/finally to unlink tmp.

main.py /api/system-deps Path-2 fuzzy match uses `.startswith(wanted)` with `name.replace('-', ' ')` — for `webview-138` this matches any apk whose name starts with 'webview 138' regardless of who registered it. This is a surface-area bug for unauth'd public callers. Recommend Path 2 be admin-opt-in only.

require_admin returning bare `return` makes ALL /api/admin/* endpoints publicly callable in the running pod and on the production VPS if the same image is deployed. This is broader than this bundle's scope but worth re-stating because it affects /api/admin/system-deps/* directly.

ApkmInstaller.kt (static review): `download()` should validate Content-Length and stream with size cap to prevent malicious/oversized bundles from filling /data — currently reads the whole stream into cacheDir without ceiling. (Not testable in this pod.)
test_credentials: Launcher admin (preview): token=onnow-launcher-admin-dev (used as Bearer header). Note: require_admin is currently a no-op so token isn't enforced.
seed_data_creation: Temporary: 64-byte dummy .apkm + transient apks/system_deps store entries during 3 test classes. All cleaned up post-test (store.json restored from backup snapshot; dummy files unlinked).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/launcher-backend/tests/test_apkm_system_deps.py. Run with `cd /app && python -m pytest launcher-backend/tests/test_apkm_system_deps.py -v`. Launcher backend on port 8002 (supervisor: launcher-backend). require_admin is intentionally disabled (see main.py:515). Native Android Kotlin is build-only — cannot run in this pod.
rca of the issue: n/a — no critical bugs found; one auth-disabled note already documented in source.
